questions.input: What is one main reason the semiconductor industry is shifting towards 3D heterogenous integration?

It enables continued performance scaling beyond the limits of Mooreâ€™s Law
It eliminates the need for thermal management in high-density systems
It replaces the need for packaging altogether

questions.input: Which statement about Fan-Out Wafer-Level Packaging (FO-WLP) is correct?

questions.answer: Artificially freeze sea ice to make up for ice melting in the Arctic ice cap

questions.input: How does Real Ice artificially freeze sea water?

By seeding clouds to lower atmospheric temperature in polar regions
By installing reflective panels on ice sheets to reduce heat absorption
By deploying undersea refrigeration units to cool the Arctic Ocean

questions.answer: By pumping sea water on top of existing ice, where the cold air freezes it

questions.input: Which of the following best describes the engineering logic behind pumping seawater onto Arctic ice during winter?

The water ran off into crevasses rather than freezing.
It formed a dense, blue slush layer that started to freeze within hours.
Wind dispersed it before it could accumulate.
The water flash-froze into brittle ice crystals.

BJTs use both majority and minority carriers; FETs use only majority carriers
BJTs operate at higher voltages than FETs

questions.input: In an NPN transistor, what type of charge carriers primarily control current flow?
questions.answer: Electrons

questions.input: What is the role of the Gate terminal in a FET?
options: Connects the device to power supply
Controls the size and shape of the channel between Source and Drain
questions.answer: Controls the size and shape of the channel between Source and Drain

questions.input: Which type of transistor is preferred for fast switching and high input impedance?
questions.answer: MOSFET

questions.input: Why are N‐channel MOSFETs used more often than P‐channel MOSFETs?
options: Electrons have higher mobility than holes
questions.answer: Electrons have higher mobility than holes

questions.input: Which of the following transistors is sensitive to light instead of electric signals?
questions.answer: Photo Transistor

questions.input: Which transistor type is not suitable for signal amplification?
questions.answer: UJT (Uni-Junction Transistor)

questions.input: What does the hFE value on a small signal transistor indicate?
questions.answer: Signal amplification capacity

questions.input: Which type of transistor is commonly used in high‐frequency switching applications like CATV and UHF systems?
questions.answer: High Frequency (RF) Transistor

questions.input: Which of the following best explains why BJTs are classified as "bipolar" devices?
options: They contain both P‐type and N‐type materials
They can operate in either switching or amplifying modes
They rely on both majority and minority charge carriers for conduction
They require both voltage and current for activation
They include both enhancement and depletion regions
questions.answer: They contain both P-type and N-type materials

questions.input: What structural characteristic distinguishes MOSFETs from JFETs?
options: MOSFETs have only two terminals while JFETs have three
JFETs use electron flow while MOSFETs use hole flow exclusively
MOSFETs feature an insulated gate separated by a thin oxide layer
JFETs operate using the photovoltaic effect
MOSFETs require minority carrier injection to switch states
questions.answer: MOSFETs feature an insulated gate separated by a thin oxide layer

questions.input: Why do MOSFETs typically offer higher input impedance than BJTs?
options: The gate in MOSFETs is insulated, preventing direct current flow
MOSFETs use thermal gates that resist AC frequencies
BJTs lack a proper emitter region
BJTs are voltage‐controlled and lack impedance variation
questions.answer: The gate in MOSFETs is insulated, preventing direct current flow

questions.input: Which of the following scenarios would most likely require a photo‐FET instead of a photo‐BJT?
options: A system requiring a fast on/off switching in the absence of light
An application demanding high light sensitivity and voltage‐based control
A situation with frequent thermal cycling
A circuit using only small signal amplification
Any system using analog voltage regulation
questions.answer: An application demanding high light sensitivity and voltage‐based control

questions.input: What is the most likely reason N‐channel MOSFETs are more commonly used than P‐channel types in modern circuits?
options: N‐channel devices are cheaper to fabricate with higher voltage thresholds
P‐channel devices have better performance in high‐temperature environments
Electrons in N‐channel devices offer superior mobility over holes in P‐channel devices
N‐channel MOSFETs are less affected by input impedance
P‐channel MOSFETs require no gate voltage to operate
questions.answer: Electrons in N‐channel devices offer superior mobility over holes in P‐channel devices

questions.input: Which region of operation in a BJT corresponds to its use as a linear amplifier?
questions.answer: Active region

questions.input: Which of the following differentiates enhancement‐mode MOSFETs from depletion‐mode ones?
options: Enhancement‐mode requires a gate voltage to form a conductive channel; depletion‐mode conducts by default
Depletion‐mode MOSFETs are always made from polycrystalline silicon
Enhancement‐mode MOSFETs do not use any doping in the substrate
Only enhancement‐mode MOSFETs operate in high‐frequency applications
Depletion‐mode transistors use minority carriers for conduction
questions.answer: Enhancement‐mode requires a gate voltage to form a conductive channel; depletion‐mode conducts by default

questions.input: Which statement most accurately reflects the role of gate voltage in a JFET?
options: It generates a charge imbalance in the emitter‐base junction
It controls the depletion layer in the gate‐collector interface
It varies the width of the conducting channel between source and drain
It amplifies the base signal voltage directly
It activates the substrate for dual‐mode conduction
questions.answer: It varies the width of the conducting channel between source and drain

questions.input: A designer needs a transistor capable of handling 15 A of collector current and operating near 100 MHz. Which transistor is most appropriate?
questions.answer: Power transistor
